jobs.title: Structural Engineering Intern at Ozer Engineering
jobs.description: Employer: Ozer Engineering Expires: 08/15/2026 Paid Structural Engineering Intern – Field Experience Memphis, TN | Field-Based, Hybrid (Field & Office) | Fall 2026 — 8/10/2026 Start Date | Approx. 15-20 hrs/week, flexible around class scheduleOverviewOzer Engineering is a Memphis structural engineering firm delivering both new construction design and adaptive reuse of existing buildings — from historic rehabilitation to arts and cultural spaces to mobility and bridge projects across Memphis and the Tri-State area (North Mississippi, East Arkansas, West Tennessee). We design, assess, repair, strengthen, and design structures across a wide range of building types and materials. Licensed in Arkansas, California, Mississippi, Tennessee, and Texas, Ozer works with diverse projects to create sound structures that are resilient in an era of rapid climate change. We are hiring one paid intern to work directly with a licensed Professional Engineer (PE) on real building assessments across Memphis and the Tri-State area). This role is built for someone who wants to learn structural engineering through hands-on field experience — not just classroom theory or desk work.Please note: Candidates must be located within a reasonable commute of our Memphis office. This is a seasonal 4-month) internship; we do not provide travel assistance or reimburse relocation expenses.What You'll DoParticipate in site investigations of residential and commercial buildingsDocument structural conditions using notes, photos, measurements, and sketchesAssist in evaluating structural issues and developing practical recommendationsSupport the preparation of engineering reports and client-facing documentationObserve construction and how engineering decisions are implementedFieldwork includes attics, crawlspaces, roofs, and active construction sites.What Makes This Role DifferentDirect mentorship from a licensed PEReal field experience, not just modeling or desk workExposure to real buildings, real problems, and real engineering judgmentFlexible hours to accommodate academic schedulesOffice located at 88 Union in Downtown Memphis, near restaurants, the FedEx Forum, and a short walk to Riverfront Park — visible from our office windowsWhat You Get$16–$20/hour based on experienceFlexible scheduling around your academic commitmentsMileage reimbursement and parking coveredMeal allowance for out-of-town site visitsA fast-paced team that knows how to enjoy the moment Monthly Lunch and Learn Events: Learn from over 30 years of structural experience while enjoying a delicious meal on us!Reserved covered parking spotHands-on experience in structural assessments and technical reportingTuition reimbursement of up to $500 upon successful completion of the internship (discretionary)What You'll Have a Chance to LearnTechnical / Industry-SpecificIdentify and describe common structural systems and load paths in residential and small commercial buildingsConduct field observations and document structural conditions using notes, photographs, and sketchesDistinguish between typical construction deficiencies and potential structural concernsAssist in evaluating structural issues and understand how engineering recommendations are developedGain familiarity with industry tools used for documentation and assessment (e.g., Revit, CompanyCam, AirTable, ArcSite)Observe how structural engineering recommendations are implemented during constructionTransferable / ProfessionalCommunicate technical observations clearly in written reports and summaries for clients and project teamsExplain structural issues in a clear, accessible way to non-technical audiencesDemonstrate professional judgment by asking questions, seeking clarification, and escalating concerns appropriatelyManage time effectively while balancing fieldwork, documentation, and deadlinesWork collaboratively with engineers, contractors, and clients in a professional environmentDevelop attention to detail through accurate on-site documentationEssential SkillsStatics & materials — working knowledge of statics (load paths, forces, free body diagrams) and material behavior (wood, steel, concrete, masonry) under loadField observation & documentation — accurate notes, measurements, photos, and sketches on-siteTechnical writing — translating field findings into clear reports for contractors and property ownersEngineering judgment — recognizing when something looks wrong and knowing what to askDrawing literacy — reading and marking up structural drawings; AutoCAD, ArcSite, or Revit familiarity a plusVerbal communication — clear, professional in client- and contractor-facing settingsPhysical capability for confined-space fieldwork — entering and maneuvering through crawlspaces as narrow as 18 inches high, often in damp or muddy conditions, on hands and knees or by low-crawling; also attics, roofs, and active construction sites. (We use a crawling robot when a space is too tight to access safely, but physically entering and working in tight, low crawlspaces is a routine, essential part of this job.)QualificationsCivil or Structural Engineering student (preferred: final year), recent graduate, or STEM OPT candidateCoursework in statics and materials (mechanics of materials) completed or in progressInterest in structural engineering and existing buildingsComfortable working in field environments (attics, crawlspaces, construction sites)Valid driver's license and reliable transportationDon't meet every qualification? We'd still like to hear from you — the skills that matter most for this role are statics, field judgment, communication, and a willingness to learn.Work AuthorizationCandidates must be authorized to work in the United States. Students authorized under CPT, OPT, or STEM OPT are welcome to apply. Ozer does not sponsor employment visas.Our ValuesOur work is anchored in practical engineering judgment, clear communication, and responsible enhancements to existing structures. We believe engineers learn best by studying real buildings and gaining hands-on experience.We are a collaborative, team-driven firm with diverse professionals and women in leadership. We value different perspectives — they strengthen our work and our team. Ozer Engineering is an equal opportunity employer; we consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other status protected by applicable law.Application ProcessApplications reviewed on a rolling basis — apply by 7/31/2026 for priority consideration. Selected candidates will be invited to a 30-minute interview. We aim to move quickly and respect your time throughout the process.Growth OpportunitySuccessful interns may be considered for future roles; however, participation in the internship does not guarantee employment.

jobs.feedName: Andrew J. Anagnost College of Engineering and Computer Science
jobs.title: Quality Engineer Intern at Cast21
jobs.description: Employer: Cast21 Expires: 08/15/2026 Cast21 is a rapidly growing medical device startup whose mission is to change the way the world heals, starting with broken bones. We combine advanced materials science with user-friendly design to redefine the healing experience for patients and providers. Our team is committed to developing safe, effective products for orthopedic applications. Creative and clever people with a passion for change drive the company’s success. We are seeking a paid, part-time Quality Engineer (Intern.) This intern will have hands-on exposure to manufacturing engineering, process improvement, and production operations. This position is in-person in Evanston, IL with less than 25% of the role being remote. The intern will improve day-to-day manufacturing processes across production, assist with process troubleshooting, and complete reports with the production and operations teams. The ideal candidate is curious, analytical, and creative, with an interest in improving real-world manufacturing systems. Exceptional candidates will have the opportunity to take ownership of processes, develop KPI-driven improvements, and see how their contributions directly impact throughput, quality, and unit economics. This role includes paid training and is expected to last for at least two terms (approximately 500 hours.) Total hours per week are flexible and will be mutually agreed upon between the candidate and Director of Operations. Some Roles and Potential ResponsibilitiesImprove manufacturing processes and work instructionsAnalyze production data for efficiency and quality improvementsAuthor and approve quality documentsSupport continuous improvement and yield optimization projectsCollaborate with R&D, quality, and operations teamsLead process improvement projects tied to manufacturing KPIsMinimum Requirements for this PositionCurrently enrolled student (engineering, operations, or related field preferred)Standing or walking for extended periodsAbility to lift and carry up to 50 lbsStrong integrity and professionalismDetail-oriented with good observational skillsStrong verbal and written communication skillsGoogle Drive (sheets and docs)

jobs.title: Software Engineer – 2027 Internship Program (June Start) at Old Mission
jobs.description: Employer: Old Mission Expires: 08/15/2026 Old Mission is a global proprietary trading firm that leverages state-of-the-art technology and research to identify and execute profitable trading strategies across multiple asset classes around the world. Our offices in Chicago, New York, and London are all composed of naturally-curious individuals who thrive in a team environment and constantly strive for improvement.Old Mission does not seek capital from outside investors, allowing us the flexibility to aggressively invest in our team members and keep them engaged in the firm’s growth.About the PositionOur internship program is geared for current students who want to work alongside our talented software engineers creating impactful solutions to further the firm's market initiatives. We are a smaller firm, which means you'll get to know the people you're working with and they'll get to know you. Responsibilities include supporting the development of C++ based trading applications and tools. You will be given hands-on projects that contribute to the firm's daily activities, giving you real exposure to how our business runs, not just observation. In this role, you'll have the opportunity to learn more about creative programming and the tools needed to support electronic trading. If you're looking to make an immediate contribution this summer and get a firsthand look at building out our business, feel free to apply today!What you will get to doGain exposure to how trading works, from the underlying theory to the systems and tools we build around itRegular sessions with senior engineers covering the fundamentals of the systems and tools we useGet direct feedback from the engineers you're working alongside, learning in real timeWork directly with engineers, traders, and other teams on projects that matter to the firm day to dayRequired SkillsCurrently pursuing a bachelor's or master's in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a similar technical fieldMinimum 3.0 GPA A strong work ethic and ability to adapt quickly in a fast-paced environmentAt least junior standing, with a graduation date of December 2027 or May 2028Comfortable with one or more of C++, Python or TypeScriptA solid grasp of computer science fundamentals: data structures, algorithms, systemsGenuine interest in markets and how software supports themAbility to start the internship on June 1, 2027No finance experience is requiredBenefits and PerksFully stocked kitchen; free breakfast and lunch every day on-siteCorporate housing at no cost to youAccess to people across the firm — traders, researchers, and engineers

jobs.description: Employer: Recon RF, Inc. In these cutting-edge fields, we seek ambitious and highly motivated Electrical Engineers who have demonstrated high-proficiency in circuit design, board layout, and testing to join our innovative team - a great place to learn and master new advanced skills in our high-growth, very high-tech environment where the work is hard but you'll learn a lot- while pioneering the future of advanced technology. ensure accurate data collection for each DUT type.Load-Pull & Noise-Parameter Testing:Proficiency in load-pull and noise-parameter testing is preferred, with training provided as needed.Technical Analysis & Documentation:Ability to read, write, and document technical information effectively.Complete comprehensive technical analyses and generate detailed test reports.High computer literacy required RF Lab Etiquette:Familiar with proper RF-microwave lab etiquette, tool usage, cleanliness, and RF torque wrenches.Proficiency with both in-fixture (coax and waveguide) and on-wafer testing.Strong mechanical aptitude and adherence to RF lab tool etiquette.Test Software Proficiency:Proficiency in test software tools such as Python (preferred), Matlab, LabVIEW, etc.Requirements:Education and/or Experience: preferred, but not required for exceptional candidates: Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, RF EngineeringTraining can be provided for exceptional candidates that have basic RF-Testing experienceRF-Concepts: Strong understanding of RF concepts like Return-Loss/VSWR, gain, power gain, Noise Figure, Noise Temperature, dBm, Watts, S-Parameters, Linearity, Smith-Chart, and complex numbers.Technical Reading/Writing: Ability to read and write well, with strong technical analysis and documentation skills.What We Offer:A fast-paced, high-tech startup environment.Opportunities for growth and career advancement as the company expands.Competitive salary and benefits package.Benefits:Health Insurance StipendProfessional Development: jobs.feedName: Andrew J. Anagnost College of Engineering and Computer Science
jobs.title: College Co-op MSD Wastewater Engineering/LiDAR at City of Cincinnati
jobs.description: Employer: City of Cincinnati - Metropolitan Sewer District Expires: 08/16/2026 MSD WASTEWATER ENGINEERING - (GIS/Acoustic Sound/LiDar) Mission: Learn the value of data collection and its applicability to industrial uses, as well as the limitations and challenges of budget and time constraints. Tackle through a hands-on experience, the philosophical question of how to balance the cost to obtain information and its quality vs. the value it provides.Purpose: Develop software application tools to improve response time and reduce costs for data collection related to infrastructure condition assessment. Apply applications to sensors and micro-computers which can be used in the field take measurements in the environments of MSD assets. If time permits, work on ArcMap GIS applications and develop Python routines to automate desired, repetitive tasks performed by MSD users. Overview: Work as part of a team in a professional engineering environment. Develop communication skills both with engineers as well as maintenance staff. Learn about condition assessment and the factors that affect and age infrastructure. Learn about civil engineering and sewer design. Assume responsibility for ethical action and work production.Expectations: Via small, inexpensive LiDAR units, collect and record data to a Raspberry Pi micro-computer to create 3D point clouds of interior spaces of pipes and structures. Include and apply additional peripheral devices such as position trackers, range finders, possible other devices. Develop an application to visually represent 3D point clouds which is intuitive, easy to review, and allows the user to determine dimensions of a structure at a chosen point in the structure as determined by user input. Determine typical levels of error in collected data. Remain flexible and adaptable due to a myriad of dynamic influences including the following: budget, time constraints, reviews and requests from engineers or maintenance staff, equipment limitations, other organizational needs, etc. As time allows, develop mounts with a 3D printer, so the various components of sensors can be attached together, forming a single, overall unit. Be resourceful in eliminating bugs in code and communication between devices and operating systems. All intellectual property that results from development, including authorship of code, will be considered public domain, to be copied, published, and utilized for use as deemed fit by the public. Deliverables Co-op to deliver:-Python code to run sensors and collect data through a micro-computer on a Linux OS.-Python code to generate intuitive images of 3D point clouds, both in a Linux and Microsoft OS.-Python code for users to determine structure dimensions, anywhere within its 3D point cloud.-Documentation of how code works and how to set up micro-computers, sensors, and output. MSD to provide:-Budget: desktop or laptop computer; micro-computers (Raspberry Pi), LiDAR units, movement tracking sensors, salary of student (roughly 15 weeks).-Logistics: workstation in the WEEC Building.-Training: training on city policies, rules, regulations. Milestones Orientation (week #1) Needs Assessment (week #2) Begin Project Work (weeks #1 - 3) Project Manager and co-op will work together to develop a working document that defines projects and timelines that ensures that project goals and objectives will be achieved. The projects will be tailored to suit the student’s interests as available. Continuation of Project Work (weeks #4 - 14) Investigate solutions, gather information from various sources, and work as necessary to complete project deliverables as well as possible additional ideas on project solutions. Perform mid-term review of project schedule and timeline at week #8. Make necessary adjustments to meet project objectives and project manager’s expectations. Prepare Final Documentation and Report Out (weeks #15 - 16) Conclude projects, where possible. Provide documentation and discuss with necessary parties to enable continuation of unfinished work. Summarize and evaluate lessons learned. Education: The applicant must currently be a college student with successful completion of college classes in computer science. The applicant must possess the ability to develop and author computer processing tools with Python code to crunch large volumes of data using math and science parameters. Below, a more detailed list of desired skillsets is provided. Required:The ability to author and implement Python code to collect, process, and export coordinate datasets using trigonometric functions.Use of Python and math to create intuitive, easily discernible, yet accurate and informative visual representation of coordinate data clouds.Completion, as a minimum, Calculus I with an overall grade of “A-”, or better (or equivalent).A collegiate math and science cumulative GPA of 3.4, (on a 4.0, unweighted scale), or better.Familiarity with Linux as well as Microsoft operating systems, environments, and parameters.Ability to import and export large datasets across a micro-computer vs. more powerful computing platform (desktop or laptop computer).Ability to set up Linux operating systems on micro-computers, then install and run Python code.Ability to set up environments on micro-computers and install peripheral devices for sensing and data collection which can communicate with the micro-computer and the code written by the applicant.Familiarity with computer blogs, or other computer science community resources.Resourcefulness in finding solutions when sensors fail to communicate or work as intended. Desired, but not required: Ability to utilize artificial intelligence and algorithms for use with analyzing video imagery.Familiarity with electronics and power requirements including resistance, voltage, and amperage.Experience tutoring or teaching.Familiarity with 3D printing.Familiarity with Esri ArcMap. HR Contact: Jerrold Ware, Jerrold.Ware@cincinnati-oh.gov
